footer.copyrightNote: Scheme text belongs to the Government of India and is reproduced from public official sources. No personal data is collected or stored.
footer.disclaimer: Adhikaar explains eligibility using official scheme documents. It is informational only — not legal advice — and final decisions rest with the implementing authorities. Always confirm on the official portal before acting.

wizardChrome.reviewLead: A quick look before we prepare your report. You can change anything.
wizardChrome.defaultLead: Answer what you can — “Not sure” is always allowed, and nothing is stored.
wizardChrome.resumeHelp: Paste the code you copied earlier to pick up where you left off.
wizardChrome.resumeInvalid: That code doesn't look right. Check it and try again.
wizardChrome.resumeWelcome: Welcome back — your answers are restored.
wizardChrome.codeCopied: Resume code copied — paste it here any time to continue.
wizardChrome.fillRequired: Please fill this in before continuing.
wizardChrome.chooseAnswer: Please choose an answer — “Not sure” is always fine.
wizardChrome.numberRange: Please enter a number between {min} and {max}.

results.preparingBody: Checking your answers against the official rules of {count} schemes — this usually takes under half a minute.
results.entitledTitleOne: You may be entitled to 1 scheme
results.entitledTitleMany: You may be entitled to {count} schemes
results.noEntitlementTitle: We couldn't confirm an entitlement yet
results.entitledLead: Based on what you told us and the official rules, here is where you stand — including exactly why, what to bring, and where to apply.
results.moreInfoLead: A few schemes need one more detail each before we can say — they're listed below with exactly what to confirm.
results.noneLead: Based on what you told us, none of the {count} covered schemes matched — the reasons are below, and the rules they rest on are linked.
results.linkCopied: Link copied — anyone with it sees this report, so share with care.
results.linkCopyFailed: Couldn't copy automatically — you can copy the address bar instead.
results.entitledSectionLead: Verdicts marked “likely” rest on facts the office will verify, like the BPL list.
results.moreInfoSectionLead: We'd rather ask than guess. Each card says exactly what to confirm.
results.notEligibleSectionLead: Each card shows the specific rule that wasn't met — circumstances change, and so can this.
results.emptyBody: Tell us about your situation first — it takes about three minutes, and nothing is stored.
results.errorFallback: Something unexpected went wrong. Please try again.
results.needInfoTitle: One more thing before your report
results.needInfoFallback: Could you share a little more about your age, work, and family income?
results.checklistLead: Everything the schemes above ask for, in one list — tick items off as you collect them. Printed, the empty boxes make a checklist you can carry to the office.

whatIf.title: What if things were different?
whatIf.lead: Try a different age, income, or a BPL card and see which verdicts would flip — checked against the same official rules. Your report above stays untouched, and nothing is stored.
whatIf.noChange: No verdicts change with these values.
whatIf.error: The simulation couldn't run. Please try again.

feedback.title: Report a problem with this report
feedback.lead: Spotted a wrong verdict, a bad translation, or an outdated document list? Tell us — it goes straight to the maintainers.
feedback.noPii: Please don't include your name, phone, or any ID numbers — reports are anonymous, and identifiers are removed automatically.
feedback.sent: Thank you — your report was recorded.
feedback.error: Your report couldn't be sent. Please try again.

card.why: Why — grounded in the official text
card.beforeApply: Before you apply — this verdict rests on facts the office will verify:

chips.heading: Based on what you told us
chips.ariaLabel: The facts this report is based on

about.title: About you
about.lead: Most schemes depend on age and life situation — this is the foundation of your report.

place.title: Where you live
place.lead: A few schemes work differently in villages and cities.

work.title: Your work and income
work.lead: This helps us check farmer, vendor, artisan, and income-linked schemes.

work-details.title: A little more about your work

household.title: Your household
household.lead: These answers decide the housing, health, and cooking-gas schemes.

family.title: You and your family
family.lead: Last set — pensions, insurance, savings, and health cover.

age.label: Your age
age.help: In years. Most schemes have an age window, so this matters a lot.
age.errorText: Please enter your age — most schemes depend on it.

gender.label: Gender
gender.whyWeAsk: Some schemes are only for women — like the widow pension and the daughters' savings scheme. We never store this.

marital_status.label: Marital status
marital_status.whyWeAsk: The Indira Gandhi Widow Pension depends on marital status. We only use this to check that one rule.

state.label: State or union territory
state.help: We currently cover central government schemes, which apply across India.

area.label: Do you live in a village or a city?
area.help: Housing support, for example, is currently for rural areas.

occupation.label: What best describes your main work?

annual_family_income_inr.label: Your family's yearly income (₹)
annual_family_income_inr.help: Everyone in the household together, before tax. A rough estimate is fine.
annual_family_income_inr.whyWeAsk: Several schemes have an income ceiling — for example ₹2.5 lakh a year for the SC scholarship. Your answer is only compared against those limits and never stored.

pays_income_tax.label: Did anyone in your family pay income tax last year?
pays_income_tax.help: Some schemes exclude income-tax payers. “Not sure” is a fine answer.

is_farmer_with_land.label: Does your family own cultivable land in its own name?
is_farmer_with_land.help: PM-KISAN support goes to landholding farmer families.

cultivates_crops.label: Do you grow crops on that land?
cultivates_crops.help: Crop insurance applies to farmers actually growing notified crops.

has_land_ownership_or_tenure_docs.label: Do you have land papers — ownership or tenancy documents?

holds_constitutional_or_political_post.label: Has anyone in your family held a constitutional post or elected office — minister, MP, MLA, mayor, or district panchayat chairperson?
holds_constitutional_or_political_post.help: PM-KISAN excludes families of such office-holders.

is_practicing_registered_professional.label: Is anyone in your family a practicing registered professional — doctor, engineer, lawyer, chartered accountant, or architect?
is_practicing_registered_professional.help: PM-KISAN excludes families of practicing registered professionals.

has_vending_certificate_or_lor.label: Do you have a vending certificate or a letter of recommendation from your town body?
has_vending_certificate_or_lor.help: PM SVANidhi loans ask for one of these. If not, your town's vending committee can issue it.

is_vishwakarma_trade_artisan.label: Is your craft one of the 18 traditional trades — like carpenter, blacksmith, tailor, potter, cobbler, or goldsmith?
is_vishwakarma_trade_artisan.help: PM Vishwakarma supports these listed family trades.

is_post_matric_student.label: Are you studying after Class 10 (post-matric)?
is_post_matric_student.help: The SC scholarship covers courses after Class 10.

social_category.label: Social category
social_category.whyWeAsk: The Post-Matric Scholarship is specifically for SC students, so we can only check it with this answer. It is never stored or shared.

has_bpl_card.label: Is your family on the BPL list, or do you hold a BPL / priority ration card?
has_bpl_card.whyWeAsk: Pension and cooking-gas schemes use the BPL list. Since we can't check the list itself, a “yes” makes those verdicts “likely eligible”, to be confirmed at the office.

house_type.label: What kind of house do you live in?
house_type.help: Rural housing support goes to families in kutcha houses.

kutcha.label: Kutcha
kutcha.description: Mud, thatch, or other temporary materials

has_lpg_connection.label: Does your household already have an LPG gas connection?

owns_motorized_vehicle.label: Does anyone in the family own a motorised vehicle — a car, tractor, or two-wheeler?
owns_motorized_vehicle.help: A few schemes use this to identify better-off households.

has_bank_account.label: Do you have a bank account?
has_bank_account.help: Insurance and pension schemes pay through a bank account.

family_member_in_govt_service.label: Is anyone in your family a serving or retired government employee?

receives_govt_pension_over_10k.label: Does anyone in the family receive a government pension of ₹10,000 or more a month?

daughter_age.label: If you have a daughter under 10, how old is she?
daughter_age.help: The Sukanya Samriddhi savings account can be opened until she turns 10. Skip this if it doesn't apply.

is_pregnant.label: Are you currently pregnant?
is_pregnant.help: Maternity benefit schemes need this to check eligibility.

single_girl_child.label: Are you the only girl child in your family (no brother)?
single_girl_child.whyWeAsk: A few scholarships are reserved for a family's single girl child. We only use this to check those, and nothing is stored.

disability_percent.label: If you live with a disability, what percentage is on your certificate?
disability_percent.whyWeAsk: The Indira Gandhi Disability Pension needs 80% or more. We ask for the number only to check that rule, and it is never stored.

disability_type.label: Which category is on the disability certificate?
disability_type.whyWeAsk: Some disability schemes apply only to specific categories named in the National Trust Act. Leave this blank if you're not sure.

options.label: Prefer not to say / not sure

bereavement_event.label: Has the main earner of your family passed away?
bereavement_event.whyWeAsk: The National Family Benefit Scheme gives a one-time payment to a poor family whose primary breadwinner has died. We ask only to check that scheme.

is_pmjay_priority_category.label: Is your family in a deprived or priority group — for example landless labourers, or listed in the SECC survey?
is_pmjay_priority_category.help: Ayushman Bharat health cover uses these categories. “Not sure” is fine — many families are listed without knowing.
